items.headline: African leaders back new guarantee mechanism to unlock investment
items.body: A summit in Nairobi secured political backing for a new pan-African financial architecture. This advances a framework to lower capital costs and accelerate job creation across Africa.

items.headline: Kuwait arrests alleged Iranian Revolutionary Guards members after sea clash
items.body: Kuwait announced it arrested four people affiliated with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. The incident introduces a new geography to the escalating regional conflict.

items.headline: EU agrees to extend compliance deadlines for its AI Act
items.body: EU bodies reached a political agreement to amend the landmark AI Act. Companies now have until at least December 2027 to comply with high-risk system rules.

items.headline: Bolivian court reissues arrest warrant for former president Evo Morales
items.body: A court declared the former leader a fugitive after he missed a trial start. His supporters are promising a nationwide insurgency if he is captured.

items.headline: Gunfire erupts in Philippine Senate during ICC arrest attempt for drug war senator
items.body: Armed confrontation broke out Wednesday as authorities tried to arrest Senator dela Rosa for crimes against humanity linked to 32 killings. Allied senators placed him in protective custody, forcing President Marcos to issue public calm appeals.

items.headline: Trump meets Xi in Beijing summit with three US tech CEOs in delegation
items.body: The presidents began two-day talks Thursday with Apple, Tesla, and Nvidia chiefs present. Xi opened by asking whether they could avoid conflict between rising and established powers.

items.headline: South Carolina Supreme Court overturns Alex Murdaugh conviction, orders new murder trial
items.body: The court ruled unanimously Wednesday that the clerk improperly influenced the jury. The state attorney general vowed immediate retrial for the killings.

items.headline: Nigerian airstrike kills over 100 civilians at crowded market in Zamfara state
items.body: Military jets struck Sunday's market gathering, the second mass-casualty market bombing in a month. Hospitals in two districts are treating dozens of wounded women and girls.
